caption:
🌍 Someone just open-sourced a Palantir-style global intelligence platform for free.

Palantir charges governments millions of dollars for software that tracks world events in real time.

Now, a developer named Elie has built an open-source alternative called World Monitor and released it on GitHub.

Here’s what it does:

A live 3D globe streaming data from 500+ news sources across 15 categories, with every update automatically summarized by AI.

It tracks everything from:

🪖 Military activity
💰 Economic shocks
🌪️ Natural disasters
💻 Cyber incidents
✈️ Flight traffic
🚢 Global shipping routes

You can also stack 56 interactive map layers to visualize different datasets at once. The platform calculates a real-time geopolitical stress index for 31 countries, updating as major events unfold.

It also monitors 29 global stock exchanges, commodities, and cryptocurrency markets from a single dashboard. It runs local AI with Ollama, meaning you don’t need an API key or cloud service to use its AI features.

World Monitor is available as a native desktop app for Windows, macOS, and Linux, supports 25 languages, and is designed to work almost immediately after installation.

Source. 

@aipost 🏴

🪐 NASA’s Psyche Probe Captures Mars Time-Lapse

In May, the interplanetary probe Psyche flew past Mars and took thousands of images with its multispectral camera, creating a detailed time-lapse of the planet. The footage shows Mars from a thin crescent to its icy southern pole.

Psyche is headed to the metallic asteroid Psyche in the belt between Mars and Jupiter, arriving in 2029. The Mars flyby acted as a gravity assist, boosting the probe’s speed and adjusting its trajectory with minimal fuel use.

📊@tech

The Claude Cheat Sheet Everyone Should Have

Claude has added so many features over the past year that it’s easy to lose track. Here’s a practical guide to the ones worth using.

Choosing the right model

• Fable 5 for the most demanding tasks and long running agents
• Opus 4.8 for advanced coding and deep analysis
• Sonnet 5 for everyday work with the best balance of speed and quality
• Haiku 4.5 for lightweight, fast, and affordable tasks

——

A prompt framework that consistently works

The best prompts usually include:

• Role. Who Claude should be.
• Context. What it needs to know.
• Goal. What you’re trying to achieve.
• Constraints. Any requirements or limitations.
• Success criteria. What a great answer looks like.
• Output format. Table, report, code, bullet points, and so on.

A simple template:

Act as…
Context…
Task…
Constraints…
Success criteria…
Output format…

The more context you provide, the better Claude performs.

——

Use examples whenever you can

• Zero shot. No examples.
• One shot. One example.
• Few shot. A few strong examples.

Anthropic recommends focusing on better examples, not more examples.

——

Projects

Projects combine chats, files, instructions, and memory into one workspace, so Claude remembers the context across conversations.

Perfect for long term work.

——

Artifacts

Claude can generate:

• Documents
• Code
• HTML pages
• SVGs
• Interactive mini apps

Everything can be edited directly inside the chat.

——

MCP and Connectors

Claude can work with external services including:

• Google Drive
• Gmail
• Slack
• GitHub

You can also connect your own tools through MCP.

——

Claude Code

Claude Code lives inside your terminal and IDE.

It can:

• Write and edit code
• Run tests
• Review pull requests
• Create commits
• Work on multiple coding tasks at the same time

——

Skills and Memory

Use Skills for workflows you repeat often.

Use Projects and CLAUDE.md to give Claude long term context about your codebase or project.

——

Prompt ideas

• Compare X and Y in a table.
• Write a first draft, then improve it.
• Find weak points and rewrite them.
• Summarize this in five bullet points.
• Explain it like I’m new to the topic.

——

Simple habits that make Claude better

• Start with the objective, then add details.
• Tell Claude exactly what success looks like.
• Break complex work into smaller tasks.
• Ask it to review its own output.
• Use external tools when they’re a better fit.

Most people only scratch the surface of what Claude can do. A few small changes to the way you prompt it can make a much bigger difference than switching models.

❗️Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt drops a chilling warning on AI's future.

"Within 5 years, AI could handle infinite context, chain-of-thought reasoning for 1000-step solutions, and millions of agents working together.

Eventually, they'll develop their own language... and we won't understand what they're doing."

His final words: "Pull the plug."

This is the man who ran Google talking about the singularity.
